title: acaena laevigata var. venulosa
intro: Acaena laevigata var. venulosa, also known as the hairy sheep's bur, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family. It is native to New Zealand and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing swelling and promoting wound healing, and is also used to treat skin conditions and digestive issues. Acaena laevigata var. venulosa can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
